_note: Slab c.900-800 BC — three HISTORICAL threads splicing onto existing nodes as new families (NEVER re-parenting an existing king). THREAD 1 ARAM-DAMASCUS (ARM_, a NEW house, already registered — Israel's great rival in Kings): a self-contained succession island rooted at its founder Rezon. Spine with '—' consorts: Rezon (c.940, threw off Solomon's yoke & founded independent Damascus, 1 Kings 11:23-25) -> Hezion -> Tabrimmon -> Ben-Hadad I (c.885, son of Tabrimmon son of Hezion per 1 Kings 15:18; hired by Asa of Judah to break Baasha of Israel) -> Ben-Hadad II / Hadadezer (c.860, the Adad-idri of the Assyrian annals; fought Ahab of Israel in 1 Kings 20 & 22 and led the Levantine anti-Assyrian coalition at QARQAR 853 BC). HAZAEL (c.842) is drawn on the spine off Ben-Hadad II via a '—' consort but FLAGGED as USURPATION, NOT descent: he murdered his master Hadadezer (2 Kings 8:15) & seized the throne; the Assyrians (Shalmaneser III's inscriptions) call him a 'son of a nobody' (mar la mammana). Hazael then ravaged Israel under Jehu & Jehoahaz (2 Kings 8:12; 10:32; 13:3,22). His actual blood son Ben-Hadad III (c.796; Assyrian personal name Hadianu / 'Mari') continued the oppression of Israel (2 Kings 13:24-25). The biblical Israelite kings named on the cards — Ahab, Jehu, Jehoahaz — are referenced in prose only; NO cross-line edges are drawn (they live in the Israel house; a marriage/parent edge would double-parent them). THREAD 2 NEO-ASSYRIA (AS_): the great kings Ashurnasirpal II, Shalmaneser III & Adad-nirari III ALREADY EXIST in 10c_assyria.json and are NOT recreated or re-parented (in 10c the chain compresses Shalmaneser III -> Adad-nirari III directly). The one missing link, Shamshi-Adad V (c.824), is added as a NEW child of the existing AS_SHALMANESER3 on a SEPARATE '—' union (Shalmaneser III already parents Adad-nirari III on his own union there, so this parallel branch introduces no double-parenting). Shamshi-Adad V is then married to Queen SAMMURAMAT (Sammu-ramat, the historical kernel of the legendary Semiramis) in a childless union: their son & Sammuramat's regency ward is the already-existing Adad-nirari III, noted in-label only — NOT re-parented by a second edge. Biblical note carried on Shalmaneser III's existing card: he took tribute from Jehu of Israel (the Black Obelisk, 841 BC). THREAD 3 EGYPT 22ND/23RD (EG_): extends the Bubastite line off the existing 22nd-Dynasty tail EG_SHOSHENQ3 (referenced by id, NOT re-parented — a leaf in 21b): Shoshenq III -> Pami (Pimay) -> Shoshenq V (son of Pami per his Year-11 Serapeum stela, acceded c.767). Separately, Pedubast I (c.826) is hung off the SAME EG_SHOSHENQ3 on a distinct '—' union as the founder of the rival 23rd Dynasty at Leontopolis/Thebes, who declared himself king in Year 8 of Shoshenq III — FLAGGED: his exact filiation is DEBATED (possibly a son of the High Priest / king Harsiese A, not of Shoshenq III), so the edge marks Egypt's FRAGMENTATION, not certain descent. Prefixes: Aram-Damascus=ARM_, Assyria=AS_, Egypt=EG_. Every child/accession year is SMALLER than its parent's chain year. Dates are conventional Aramean-king-list / Neo-Assyrian / Third-Intermediate figures and are debated (flagged). Web-verified July 2026 against Wikipedia 'Hazael', 'Ben-Hadad I', 'Hadadezer (king of Aram)', 'Shamshi-Adad V', 'Semiramis', 'Shoshenq V', 'Pedubast I', 'Twenty-third Dynasty of Egypt', plus biblicalia 'Rulers of Aram-Damascus'. No fabrication; every usurpation or uncertain-descent link flagged in-node.

families.note: ARAM-DAMASCUS (1 of 7) — the founder as root of a NEW house. Rezon (no parent in the tree; an island root) & a '—' consort; his successor Hezion as child.

a.label:
👑 Rezon (Hezion)
c.940 BC · founder of the independent kingdom of Aram-Damascus · son of Eliada; a fugitive from Hadadezer of Zobah who seized Damascus & threw off the yoke of Solomon's Israel, becoming its lifelong adversary (1 Kings 11:23-25) · often equated with the Hezion whose house the later kings descend from · historical

children.label:
👑 Hezion
c.920 BC · King of Aram-Damascus · grandfather of Ben-Hadad I in the pedigree of 1 Kings 15:18 (Ben-Hadad son of Tabrimmon son of Hezion); founder-figure of the Damascene royal house · historical

families.note: ARAM-DAMASCUS (2 of 7). ARM_HEZION & a '—' consort; his son & successor Tabrimmon as child.

children.label:
👑 Tabrimmon
c.900 BC · King of Aram-Damascus · son of Hezion & father of Ben-Hadad I (1 Kings 15:18) · historical

families.note: ARAM-DAMASCUS (3 of 7). ARM_TABRIMMON & a '—' consort; his son & successor Ben-Hadad I as child.

children.label:
👑 Ben-Hadad I
c.885 BC · King of Aram-Damascus · son of Tabrimmon · bribed by Asa of Judah to break his treaty with Baasha of Israel & invade the northern kingdom, seizing Galilean towns (1 Kings 15:18-20) · historical

families.note: ARAM-DAMASCUS (4 of 7). ARM_BENHADAD1 & a '—' consort; his successor Ben-Hadad II / Hadadezer as child. This is the Aramean king of the Assyrian annals (Adad-idri) & Ahab's rival.

children.label:
👑 Ben-Hadad II (Hadadezer / Adad-idri)
c.860 BC · King of Aram-Damascus · besieged Samaria & warred with Ahab of Israel, who defeated him twice then spared him (1 Kings 20), before dying at Ramoth-Gilead in the third war (1 Kings 22) · the Adad-idri of the Assyrian records who led the twelve-king Levantine coalition against Shalmaneser III at the battle of QARQAR, 853 BC · historical

families.note: ARAM-DAMASCUS (5 of 7) — the usurper. ARM_BENHADAD2 & a '—' consort; Hazael as successor. FLAGGED USURPATION, NOT DESCENT: Hazael was a court official who smothered his master Hadadezer with a wet cloth & seized the throne (2 Kings 8:15); Shalmaneser III's inscriptions brand him a 'son of a nobody' (mar la mammana). Drawn on the spine for continuity only.

children.label:
👑 Hazael
c.842 BC · King of Aram-Damascus · a USURPER, NOT of the royal line — an officer who murdered Hadadezer & took the throne, foretold to Elijah & wept over by Elisha (2 Kings 8:7-15); the Assyrian 'son of a nobody' · Damascus's most powerful king: he ravaged Israel under Jehu & Jehoahaz, seizing all Transjordan (2 Kings 10:32-33; 13:3,22) & pressing to the gates of Gath & Jerusalem (2 Kings 12:17) · likely author of the Tel Dan Stele boasting of killing kings of Israel & Judah · historical (usurpation flagged)

families.note: ARAM-DAMASCUS (6 of 7) — the close of this slab. ARM_HAZAEL & a '—' consort; his TRUE blood son & successor Ben-Hadad III as child (2 Kings 13:24 — 'Hazael died, and Ben-Hadad his son reigned in his stead'). Real descent, unlike Hazael's own accession.

children.label:
👑 Ben-Hadad III (Hadianu / Mari')
c.796 BC · King of Aram-Damascus · son of Hazael · continued to oppress Israel under Jehoahaz, reducing its army to a remnant (2 Kings 13:3-7), until Jehoahaz's son Jehoash recovered the lost towns in three victories over him (2 Kings 13:25) · named 'Mari' in the Zakkur Stele & Hadianu in the Assyrian record · historical

families.note: NEO-ASSYRIA — the missing link. AS_SHALMANESER3 (existing king in 10c, referenced by id, NOT re-parented — he already parents Adad-nirari III there on his own union) & a NEW '—' consort; his son Shamshi-Adad V as child on this SEPARATE parallel union, so no existing king is double-parented.

children.label:
👑 Shamshi-Adad V
c.824 BC · Neo-Assyria · son of Shalmaneser III · his reign opened with a bitter CIVIL WAR against his brother Ashur-danin-pal, who had led much of Assyria in revolt against their aged father; he prevailed with Babylonian help, then campaigned against Babylon & the Zagros · historical

families.note: NEO-ASSYRIA — Queen Sammuramat / Semiramis, a CHILDLESS union by design. AS_SHAMSHIADAD5 ● Sammu-ramat (his queen). Their son is the ALREADY-EXISTING Adad-nirari III (10c), for whom she served as regent; he is noted in-label ONLY and is NOT re-parented here (no second edge). Empty children = the marriage itself, drawn to anchor the historical Semiramis.

b.label:
👑 Sammu-ramat (Semiramis)
c.820 BC · queen of Shamshi-Adad V & mother of Adad-nirari III (already in the tree; not re-drawn here) · she wielded rare power for an Assyrian queen, serving as REGENT during her son's minority & even named on a royal boundary stele beside him · the historical kernel of the later Greek legend of the warrior-queen SEMIRAMIS · historical

families.note: EGYPT 22ND DYNASTY (1 of 2). EG_SHOSHENQ3 (existing 22nd-Dyn tail in 21b, referenced by id, NOT re-parented — a leaf there) & a '—' consort; his son & successor Pami as child.

children.label:
👑 Pami (Pimay)
c.773 BC · 22nd Dynasty · son of Shoshenq III · a short reign of about six years over a Delta kingdom already shrinking as rival Libyan chiefs asserted themselves · historical

families.note: EGYPT 22ND DYNASTY (2 of 2). EG_PAMI & a '—' consort; his son & successor Shoshenq V as child (filiation fixed by Shoshenq V's Year-11 Serapeum stela).

children.label:
👑 Shoshenq V
c.767 BC · 22nd Dynasty · son of Pami · reigned about 38 years, one of the longest of the dynasty, but ruled only a rump Tanite realm as the Delta splintered among local princes (Tefnakht of Sais, Iuput II of Leontopolis, Nimlot of Hermopolis) · historical

families.note: EGYPT 23RD DYNASTY — the splintering, off the SAME 22nd-Dyn tail on a distinct union. EG_SHOSHENQ3 & another '—' consort; Pedubast I as child, founder of the rival Leontopolis 23rd Dynasty. FLAGGED: his exact filiation is DEBATED (possibly a son of the High Priest / king Harsiese A, not of Shoshenq III) — the edge marks Egypt's FRAGMENTATION, not certain descent.

children.label:
👑 Pedubast I
c.826 BC · founder of the rival 23rd Dynasty at Leontopolis / Thebes · declared himself king in Year 8 of Shoshenq III, running a parallel Upper-Egyptian line alongside the Tanite 22nd Dynasty — the formal fracture of the Third Intermediate Period · his descent is DEBATED (possibly son of Harsiese A, not of Shoshenq III); edge shows the break, not certain paternity · historical (filiation flagged)
